Document:
[color=#897236][mono]──── [color=#FFA500][bold]VI. Command Authority and Privileges[/bold][/color] ───────────────────────────────[/mono][/color]
The [bold]Chief Enforcer[/bold] holds significantly greater authority than an acting [bold]Commander[/bold]. The [bold]Chief Enforcer[/bold] also inherits all privileges granted to a [bold]Commander[/bold] by default.
An acting [bold]Commander[/bold] may not use any Chief Enforcer-specific privileges.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]Commander Privileges[/color][/head]
A [bold]Commander[/bold] has the following privileges: - 1. Change the sector's DEFCON level. - 2. Declare a No-Fly Zone. - 3. Write and sign search or arrest warrants. - 4. The ability to approve lawful executions.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]Chief Enforcer Privileges[/color][/head]
The [bold]Chief Enforcer[/bold] has the following privileges: - 1. Sign and approve Class 2 Contraband permits. - 2. Conduct warrantless searches when sufficient reasonable suspicion exists. - 3. Open new TDF positions outside of the default allotted slots established by TDF High Command. - 4. Use and access the TDF Outpost account to pay staff and fund operations. - 5. The ability to post bounties on wanted criminals, and to approve bounty payouts. - 6. Serve as the final authority in matters concerning the interpretation of TDF law, policy, and procedure whenever disputes, ambiguity, or disagreements arise.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]Bounties[/color][/head]
The Chief Enforcer has the ability to post bounties on wanted criminals. Bounties may be pursued by any vigilantes or bounty hunters within the sector. Bounties must be posted with the crime committed, who the affected parties, and a reasonable bounty fund to be paid by Auric Outpost funding. Fines collected may be used to fund bounty payouts, but assets seized may not.
[head=3][color=#FFA500]No-Fly Zones[/color][/head]
No-Fly Zones are areas declared in space, at most 250m, where civilian ships are not permitted. No-Fly Zones can only be declared by the Commander, and may only be used for the following reasons: - To protect TDF assets, operations, or personnel. - Public safety. - Intentions by citizens to access the contents of an area for illegal activity, such as smuggling.
No-Fly-Zones should not be declared to simply ward off areas arbitrarily.
When a No-Fly Zone is declared, it must be clearly stated by the Commander over Broadband or a Communications Console, preferably a Communications Console. In the announcement, they must state the duration of the No-Fly Zone, the reason for its declaration, and the name or coordinates of the restricted space and the radius of the zone.
Violating a No-Fly Zone is considered §TS-302, Traffic Noncompliance. §TS-205, Breaking and Entering may also apply if the perpetrator used force to enter a No-Fly Zone.
